reason: Both answers converge on the same core recommendation: bring in a contractor to stabilize and migrate, skip a full-time replacement hire now, move to managed Kubernetes, and hire a platform engineer later. Both correctly note the bus-factor risk of a single hire and the disproportionate ops load of 40 services for 10 engineers. Neither raises service consolidation explicitly. Answer A adds useful edge-case caveats (stabilize before migrating if fragile, on-prem/compliance exceptions, cost delta check) giving it a slight edge in decision-relevant nuance over the more concise but less caveated Answer B.
